# CrossWindowDragTarget: the receiving-window contract implementation

## Context
Tree line **C1** (#13158 map) â€” the T-C tranche gate and a fable-tier leaf: implementing ADR 0029 Â§2.3's `CrossWindowDragTarget` contract (formalized verbatim in the spec from the #13370 Option-4 convergence): how a window RECEIVES a drag that originated in a sibling window on the same App-Worker heap.

## The Problem
Cross-window drag is the only-Neo capability (single heap, multiple Chromium windows) and the highest-risk interaction surface: the receiving window must render previews for an item it doesn't host yet, negotiate drop zones through the SAME previewâ†’operation path as local drags (no parallel drag system â€” the inherited hard guardrail), and hand off atomically.

## The Architectural Reality
- Â§2.3's contract: the target registers per-window; drag state is worker-owned shared truth (`get_drag_state`'s substrate); previews are per-window projections; the drop commits ONE `transferItem`-class operation (C3's leaf) through the executor.
- Boundary: OS-window creation/reintegration stays #13028's (consumed, not owned).
- Placement: `src/dashboard/` beside the landed drag preview machinery (sibling fast-path).

## The Fix
One PR: the target implementation (registration, remote-drag preview binding, drop negotiation â†’ operation descriptor emission) + unit specs (registration lifecycle; preview binds worker drag state; drop emits the exact Â§2.3 descriptor shape; cancel path clean).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Contract shape matches Â§2.3's formalization field-for-field (reviewer table).
- [ ] Previews ride the EXISTING preview machinery (no parallel path â€” review-checkable).
- [ ] Drop emits one descriptor; no direct document mutation (executor discipline).
- [ ] Cancel/escape leaves both windows' projections clean (spec).
- [ ] Cross-family review.

## Out of Scope
`transferItem` op wiring (C3) Â· edge affordances (C4) Â· window spawning (#13028) Â· demo scene (G3).
